message:
Last winter, our power flickered during a nasty ice storm, and I suddenly realized my kids had no idea how to play cards. Like, legitimately didn't know Go Fish. We grabbed some candles, broke out a old deck, and honestly? Best few hours we'd had together in months. No screens, no 'are we there yet'—just laughter and terrible bluffing.

It got me thinking: being storm-ready doesn't have to mean fancy gear. Sometimes it's about rediscovering the stuff that actually fills your time when the usual distractions aren't there. Board games, books, card games—all things that live in a drawer and cost nothing to pull out.

Have you found any low-tech games or activities your family actually loves playing together?
